title: 10 Soothing Herbal Teas for Reddened Corners in Mouth Relief
intro_desc: Herbal teas for reddened corners in mouth are natural remedies that help soothe and calm irritated areas around the mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The best herbal teas for this condition include Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Calendula officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Achillea millefolium, Melissa officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Chamomilla recutita, and Hydrastis canadensis. If left untreated, reddened corners in mouth can lead to painful eating, speaking difficulties, and social isolation due to embarrassment and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that can help with reddened corners in the mouth. This happens when the corners of the mouth get irritated and become inflamed, often due to cold or windy weather, or even dry air. Echinacea purpurea tea is rich in antioxidants, which help to soothe and calm the irritated skin. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and ease pain, making it easier to eat and speak. The antiseptic properties of the tea prevent infection and promote healing, helping to speed up the recovery process. Drinking echinacea purpurea tea regularly can also boost the immune system, making it less likely for the condition to occur again in the future.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols in Aloe Vera help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth, making them good for soothing reddened corners caused by irritation, cuts, or other minor mouth injuries.

Quercetin is good for reddened corners in the mouth because it reduces inflammation and fights free radicals, which helps to heal and soothe sore and irritated tissues, and promotes a healthy recovery.
Tannins are good for reddened corners in mouth because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritation, promoting a healthy and comfortable mouth.
Cichoric acid is good for reddened corners in mouth because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for reddened corners in mouth
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal tea to help with reddened corners in the mouth, start by cutting a piece of Aloe vera leaf and scraping out the thick, green gel inside. Next, puree the Aloe gel in a blender or mash it with a spoon until it's smooth. Then, mix the Aloe gel with a cup of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. After that, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids. Finally, add a bit of honey or sugar to taste, if needed, and drink the Aloe vera tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm the reddened corners of your mouth. Be sure to use a clean and sterile environment when preparing the tea to avoid any contamination.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, can help soothe and calm redness in the mouth. This is because peppermint has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain. When you drink peppermint tea, the menthol in it can numb the area and provide a cooling sensation that can help calm the irritation. Peppermint tea can also help to reduce bad breath and kill bacteria that can cause mouth infections, which can further contribute to redness and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint tea can help to relax the muscles in your mouth and jaw, which can become tense and cause pain when you have mouth sores or irritation.

remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for reddened corners in mouth
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make aloe vera herbal tea that helps with reddened corners in the mouth, you will need a few simple ingredients. First, take 2-3 inner gel leaves from a mature aloe vera plant and remove the thick outer skin to expose the clear gel inside. Next, cut the gel into small pieces and add them to a cup of boiling water. Reduce the heat to low and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the gel to infuse into the water. Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the gel pieces. You can add a bit of honey or sugar to taste, as the aloe vera tea can be quite bitter. Let the tea cool down before drinking it. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the reddened corners in the mouth, reducing irritation and discomfort. It is recommended to drink the tea 2-3 times a day for optimal results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, has been used to help soothe and heal mouth ulcers and reddened corners in the mouth. This is because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain. The tea's active compound, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes in the mouth, which can become irritated and inflamed due to factors such as gum disease, poor oral hygiene, or certain medications. Drinking licorice root tea may help to calm the inflammation and promote healing, making it easier to recover from mouth ulcers and reddened corners. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial properties can help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate mouth ulcers. As a result, drinking licorice root tea may provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with mouth ulcers and reddened corners in the mouth.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for reddened corners in mouth
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal tea for soothing reddened corners in the mouth, start by washing the leaves of the Aloe vera plant and then cutting off a thick slice from the inside of the leaf. Remove the thick outer skin and scoop out the jelly-like substance inside. Grate or blend this aloe gel until it becomes a smooth paste. Next, boil a cup of water in a tea pot and then let it cool down for a few minutes. Add the aloe gel paste to the cooled water and stir well. Now, let the mixture simmer for about 10-15 minutes on low heat, or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the aloe flavor has infused into the water. Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the solids. Once the tea has cooled down, you can drink it as is, or add honey or lemon to taste. Drinking this Aloe vera tea regularly can help soothe and calm the reddened corners in your mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a natural remedy for reddened corners in the mouth, often caused by gingivitis or inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties in ginger help reduce swelling and ease pain. Ginger also has antibacterial properties that fight off germs and bacteria that cause infection and irritation. When you drink ginger tea, the active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, are absorbed into the bloodstream and travel to the mouth, where they work to calm down the inflammation and reduce redness. Additionally, ginger has natural antiseptic properties that help prevent infection and promote healing, making it an effective remedy for soothing reddened corners in the mouth.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for reddened corners in mouth
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal tea that helps with reddened corners in the mouth, start by harvesting fresh Aloe vera leaves, preferably from an organic source. Cut off a thick piece from the base of the leaf and peel off the outer layer to reveal the gel inside. Scoop out about 2-3 tablespoons of the clear gel and place it in a small saucepan. Add 2 cups of water and let it simmer over low heat for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the gel to infuse into the water. Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining gel particles. Discard the solids and let the tea cool down. Once cooled, you can drink the Aloe vera tea 2-3 times a day, preferably after meals, to help soothe and reduce inflammation in the mouth. This herbal tea is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the reddened corners of the mouth, promoting overall oral health and well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a herbal tea that can help with reddened corners in the mouth. This condition is often caused by inflammation, which can be due to irritation from spicy food, dry mouth, or other factors. The antibacterial properties in lemon balm tea can help reduce the bacteria that cause inflammation and infection. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can help calm down the irritated areas and reduce redness. The soothing effect of lemon balm tea can also provide relief from discomfort and pain. Moreover, lemon balm has antiviral properties that can help fight off viruses that may be contributing to the condition. Drinking lemon balm tea can help to reduce the severity of the symptoms and promote healing in the affected areas.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tea, also known as yarrow tea, is a natural remedy that can help soothe and calm reddened corners in the mouth. This is because yarrow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain. The tea is made from the flowers and leaves of the yarrow plant, which contain compounds that have been shown to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, helping to combat infection and prevent further irritation. When consumed, yarrow tea can help to bring down the inflammation and redness in the mouth, making it a good remedy for mouth ulcers, canker sores, and other mouth irritations. Drinking yarrow tea can also help to promote healing and speed up the recovery process, making it a popular natural remedy for mouth health. Yarrow tea can be made by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, and can be consumed as a warm tea several times a day to help alleviate symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal teas for reddened corners in mouth
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal tea for soothing reddened corners in the mouth, start by washing and drying a fresh aloe vera leaf. Remove the thick, green skin to expose the clear gel inside, then scoop out about 2-3 tablespoons of the gel and transfer it to a small saucepan. Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer over low heat. Let it cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the Aloe vera gel to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool down. Once it's lukewarm, use it as a mouthwash by swishing it around your mouth for 30 seconds to 1 minute, then spit it out. Repeat the process a few times a day to help reduce the redness and inflammation in the corners of your mouth.

Drinking this tea daily can help soothe and calm the reddened corners in your mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal teas for reddened corners in mouth
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal tea that helps with reddened corners in the mouth, start by washing a small aloe vera leaf under running water to remove any dirt. Then, remove the thick outer skin, and scoop out the clear gel from inside the leaf. Now, add a small amount of water to the gel and blend it into a smooth mixture. supplementary_best: A combination of herbal teas made from Aloe vera, Calendula officinalis, and Glycyrrhiza glabra may help remedy reddened corners in the mouth. Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. Calendula officinalis has antiseptic properties that can help prevent infections and promote healing. Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain. When consumed together, these herbs may work synergistically to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and provide relief from irritation and pain in the mouth. This combination is chosen because it addresses multiple aspects of the issue: the anti-inflammatory properties of Aloe vera and Glycyrrhiza glabra help to reduce swelling and pain, while the antiseptic properties of Calendula officinalis help to prevent infections.
supplementary_avoid: If you have reddened corners in your mouth, there are some herbal teas you should avoid. Sage tea is one to be cautious with. Sage contains a compound called thujone, which can increase blood flow to the mouth and make the reddening worse. You may also want to avoid tea made from yarrow. Yarrow has a similar effect to sage and can further irritate the area. Chamomile tea is another to be careful with, as it can be quite strong and may not be suitable for sensitive mouth areas. Lastly, avoid tea made from wormwood, which contains thujone just like sage. These teas may irritate the reddened areas or make them worse.

supplementary_related_ailments: People who have reddened corners in their mouth may be more likely to experience other health issues. These issues include dry mouth, which can be caused by a lack of saliva production, and can be treated with herbs like aloe vera and slippery elm. Aloe vera can help stimulate saliva production and soothe irritated tissues, while slippery elm can coat and protect the mouth and throat.  Another possible issue is gum inflammation, also known as gingivitis, which can be caused by poor oral hygiene and can be treated with herbs like myrrh and neem. Myrrh has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and fight off infection, while neem can help kill bacteria and reduce inflammation.  Additionally, people with reddened corners in their mouth may be more likely to experience tooth decay and cavities, which can be caused by a lack of saliva production and poor oral hygiene. Herbs like clove and garlic can be used to treat these conditions. People with reddened corners in their mouth may also be more likely to experience bad breath, which can be caused by a buildup of bacteria in the mouth. Herbs like peppermint and sage can be used to treat this condition. Peppermint can help stimulate saliva production and freshen breath, while sage can help kill bacteria and reduce inflammation.  Finally, people with reddened corners in their mouth may be more likely to experience mouth ulcers, which can be caused by stress, anxiety, and other factors. Herbs like licorice root and aloe vera can be used to treat these conditions. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and fight off infection, while aloe vera can help soothe irritated tissues and stimulate healing.
